title: Fix Stored XSS in Taxonomy Values
description:
**Penetration Test Finding — High Severity**

Taxonomy Values were vulnerable to Stored XSS due to missing input validation. Any user with Categories editing permissions could inject an XSS payload (e.g., `![](x)`) into a category cell. When other users visited the Metrics page, the stored payload would execute arbitrary JavaScript.

## Affected Endpoint

`/categories`

## Risk

High — attacker could execute arbitrary JavaScript, exfiltrate CSRF tokens, and compromise confidentiality and integrity of user data. Session cookies were protected by HTTPOnly attribute, limiting cookie exfiltration.

## Fix

Input validation implemented on the categories endpoint, converting malicious characters to HTML entities to prevent stored XSS.
acceptance_criteria: Taxonomy Value fields sanitize HTML and script tags on save
XSS payloads injected via category editing are neutralized before storage
Legitimate text content in category fields is preserved
Metrics page renders category values safely without executing injected scripts
middleware_changes: Input validation added to the categories endpoint to convert malicious characters to HTML entities before storage.
implementation_notes: Same approach as other XSS fixes in the penetration testing remediation cycle — HTML entity encoding applied to user-editable fields.
comment_summary: Developer fixed and deployed to staging.
